_meta.description: Uncle Silly Arcade – Age-based trivia question database

questions.question: What sound does a cow make?
questions.explanation: Cows moo! It's how they communicate with each other.
questions.hint: Think about a farm animal that gives us milk.

questions.question: How many legs does a spider have?
questions.explanation: Spiders have 8 legs. That's what makes them arachnids, not insects!
questions.hint: Count higher than 6.

questions.question: Which animal is known as man's best friend?
questions.explanation: Dogs have been loyal companions to humans for thousands of years!
questions.hint: They love to fetch sticks and wag their tails.

questions.question: What do caterpillars turn into?
questions.explanation: Caterpillars go through metamorphosis and become beautiful butterflies!
questions.hint: They make a cocoon first.

questions.question: Where do fish live?
questions.explanation: Fish live in water – oceans, rivers, lakes, and ponds!
questions.hint: You can see them when you go swimming.

questions.question: Which animal has a very long neck?
questions.explanation: Giraffes have the longest necks of any animal on Earth – up to 6 feet!
questions.hint: This tall animal lives in Africa.

questions.question: What color are polar bears?
questions.explanation: Polar bears have white fur that helps them blend into the snow in the Arctic.
questions.hint: Think about the color of snow.

questions.question: Which animal hops and has a pouch?
questions.explanation: Kangaroos are famous for hopping and carrying their babies (joeys) in a pouch!
questions.hint: This animal lives in Australia.

questions.question: What do plants need to grow?
questions.explanation: Plants need sunlight, water, and soil to grow and stay healthy!
questions.hint: Think about what you'd put in a garden.

questions.question: What color is the sky on a sunny day?
questions.explanation: The sky looks blue because of the way sunlight scatters through the atmosphere!
questions.hint: Look up outside on a clear day.

questions.question: What do you use to see things far away?
questions.explanation: A telescope uses lenses to make faraway things look much bigger and closer!
questions.hint: Scientists use this to look at stars.

questions.question: Which of these floats in water?
questions.explanation: Wood is less dense than water so it floats. Rocks and metal usually sink!
questions.hint: Think about what boats are made of.

questions.question: What is the hottest thing in our solar system?
questions.explanation: The Sun is a giant star! Its surface is about 10,000°F – incredibly hot!
questions.hint: It's that bright thing that gives us light and warmth.

questions.question: What shape has three sides?
questions.explanation: A triangle always has exactly three sides and three corners!
questions.hint: Count the corners on a slice of pizza.

questions.question: How many colors are in a rainbow?
questions.explanation: A rainbow has 7 colors: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Indigo, and Violet!
questions.hint: You can remember them with the name ROY G BIV.

questions.question: What do you call a baby dog?
questions.explanation: Baby dogs are called puppies. Baby cats are called kittens!
questions.hint: It sounds super cute.

questions.question: Which season comes after Winter?
questions.explanation: The seasons go: Winter, Spring, Summer, Autumn, and Winter again!
questions.hint: Flowers start to bloom in this season.

questions.question: What is 2 + 2?
questions.explanation: 2 + 2 = 4. If you have 2 apples and get 2 more, you have 4!
questions.hint: Count on your fingers.

questions.question: What is the name of the lion cub in 'The Lion King'?
questions.explanation: Simba is the little lion prince who grows up to be king in The Lion King!
questions.hint: His name means 'lion' in Swahili.

questions.question: In 'Finding Nemo', what type of fish is Nemo?
questions.explanation: Nemo is a clownfish – those orange and white striped fish that live in sea anemones!
questions.hint: Real clowns wear orange and white too.

questions.question: What color is Elsa's dress in Frozen?
questions.explanation: Elsa wears a sparkling blue ice dress – perfect for a Snow Queen!
questions.hint: Think about the color of ice.

questions.question: What type of animal is Dumbo?
questions.explanation: Dumbo is a baby elephant whose giant ears allow him to fly!
questions.hint: This animal has a trunk.

questions.question: Which princess has seven small friends called dwarfs?
questions.explanation: Snow White lives with the Seven Dwarfs: Grumpy, Happy, Sleepy, Bashful, Doc, Sneezy, and Dopey!
questions.hint: She eats a poison apple.

questions.question: What is the largest animal on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Blue Whale is the largest animal ever known – growing up to 100 feet long!
questions.hint: It lives in the ocean.

questions.question: Which bird cannot fly?
questions.explanation: Penguins can't fly but are amazing swimmers! Their wings evolved into powerful flippers.
questions.hint: This bird lives near ice and loves fish.

questions.question: What do you call a group of wolves?
questions.explanation: Wolves live in packs – family groups where they hunt and protect each other.
questions.hint: It's the same word used for a group of cards.

questions.question: Which animal is the fastest on land?
questions.explanation: Cheetahs can reach speeds of up to 70 mph – the fastest land animal!
questions.hint: This big cat has spots and lives in Africa.

questions.question: What is the only mammal that can truly fly?
questions.explanation: Bats are the only true flying mammals. Others only glide, not fly!
questions.hint: This animal is often associated with Halloween.

questions.question: How do sharks breathe?
questions.explanation: Sharks breathe with gills that extract oxygen from water as it flows through them.
questions.hint: Most fish breathe the same way.

questions.question: How many planets are in our solar system?
questions.explanation: There are 8 planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.
questions.hint: Pluto was downgraded to a dwarf planet in 2006.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars looks red because its surface is covered in iron oxide (rust)!
questions.hint: It's named after the Roman god of war.

questions.question: What is the name of Earth's natural satellite?
questions.explanation: The Moon has orbited Earth for about 4.5 billion years. It helps control our ocean tides!
questions.hint: We see it in the night sky – it glows white.

questions.question: Which planet has rings around it?
questions.explanation: Saturn has stunning rings made of ice and rock particles. They stretch 175,000 miles wide!
questions.hint: This planet is very large and yellowish.

questions.question: What is a shooting star?
questions.explanation: Shooting stars are actually meteors – space rocks that burn up as they enter Earth's atmosphere!
questions.hint: Make a wish when you see one!

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for water?
questions.explanation: Water is made of 2 hydrogen atoms and 1 oxygen atom. So it's H2O!
questions.hint: It has hydrogen and oxygen in it.

questions.question: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: Adults have 206 bones! Babies are born with about 270 and some fuse together as we grow.
questions.hint: It's between 200 and 210.

questions.question: What organ pumps blood around your body?
questions.explanation: Your heart beats about 100,000 times a day, pumping blood to every cell in your body!
questions.hint: You can feel it beating in your chest.

questions.question: What is the hardest natural substance on Earth?
questions.explanation: Diamonds are the hardest natural material – they can only be scratched by another diamond!
questions.hint: It's used in engagement rings.

questions.question: What process do plants use to make food from sunlight?
questions.explanation: Plants use sunlight, water, and CO2 to make their own food. That process is photosynthesis!
questions.hint: 'Photo' means light in Greek.

questions.question: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers about 63 million square miles – it's larger than all land combined!
questions.hint: It borders the west coast of the USA.

questions.question: Which continent is the largest?
questions.explanation: Asia is the largest continent! It's home to about 60% of the world's population.
questions.hint: China and India are on this continent.

questions.question: What is the capital city of France?
questions.explanation: Paris is the capital of France, famous for the Eiffel Tower, great food, and art!
questions.hint: It's known as the 'City of Love'.

questions.question: Which country has the most people in the world?
questions.explanation: India recently surpassed China as the world's most populous country with over 1.4 billion people!
questions.hint: As of 2023, it just became number one.

questions.question: On which continent is Egypt found?
questions.explanation: Egypt is in northeastern Africa, home to the amazing pyramids and the Nile River!
questions.hint: This is the second largest continent.

questions.question: In 'Toy Story', what is the cowboy toy's name?
questions.explanation: Woody is the cowboy sheriff doll who is Andy's favorite toy in Toy Story!
questions.hint: He says 'There's a snake in my boot!'

questions.question: Which movie features a fish named Dory who keeps forgetting things?
questions.explanation: Dory the Blue Tang fish has short-term memory loss in Finding Nemo – but she never gives up!
questions.hint: Just keep swimming!

questions.question: What school does Harry Potter attend?
questions.explanation: Harry Potter attends Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry in Scotland!
questions.hint: It's a magical school with four houses.

questions.question: In 'The Incredibles', what is the family's real last name?
questions.explanation: The superhero family's real last name is Parr! Bob, Helen, Violet, Dash, and baby Jack-Jack.
questions.hint: Bob's last name is also a unit of measure.

questions.question: What color is the Hulk?
questions.explanation: The Hulk turns big and green when Bruce Banner gets angry!
questions.hint: Think of the color of grass.

questions.question: How many players are on a soccer team on the field?
questions.explanation: Each soccer team has 11 players on the field, including the goalkeeper!
questions.hint: It's the same number as American football.

questions.question: What sport is played at Wimbledon?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon is the world's oldest Grand Slam tennis tournament, played in London every summer!
questions.hint: You use a racket in this sport.

questions.question: In basketball, how many points does a free throw score?
questions.explanation: A free throw is worth 1 point. Regular baskets are 2, and shots beyond the arc are 3!
questions.hint: It's the smallest amount you can score in one shot.

questions.question: Who was the first man to walk on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Neil Armstrong took the first steps on the Moon on July 20, 1969!
questions.hint: His first name is the same as a famous musician named Neil Young.

questions.question: What giant structure was built in ancient Egypt?
questions.explanation: The Egyptian Pyramids were built as tombs for pharaohs over 4,500 years ago!
questions.hint: They're still one of the world's wonders.

questions.question: What famous wall divides China from invaders?
questions.explanation: The Great Wall of China stretches over 13,000 miles and was built to protect against invasions!
questions.hint: It's one of the world's most famous landmarks.

questions.question: What instrument has black and white keys?
questions.explanation: A piano has 88 keys – 52 white and 36 black. Each key plays a different musical note!
questions.hint: You press keys to make music.

questions.question: What is the most spoken language in the world by native speakers?
questions.explanation: Mandarin Chinese has the most native speakers – over 900 million people speak it first!
questions.hint: It's spoken in the most populous country.

questions.question: Where does chocolate come from?
questions.explanation: Chocolate comes from cacao (cocoa) beans that grow on cacao trees in tropical regions!
questions.hint: The tree starts with 'coco'.

questions.question: Which country invented pizza?
questions.explanation: Pizza was invented in Italy – specifically Naples – in the 18th and 19th centuries!
questions.hint: The country shaped like a boot.

questions.question: What does a caterpillar do before becoming a butterfly?
questions.explanation: Caterpillars form a protective chrysalis and transform inside before emerging as butterflies!
questions.hint: It wraps itself up tight.

questions.question: Which animal has the longest migration on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Arctic Tern travels about 44,000 miles round trip from the Arctic to Antarctica every year!
questions.hint: It's a seabird that loves extreme cold.

questions.question: Which animal can change the color of its skin?
questions.explanation: Chameleons can rapidly change color to communicate, regulate temperature, and sometimes camouflage!
questions.hint: Its eyes can move independently in different directions.

questions.question: What is the gestation period of an elephant?
questions.explanation: Elephants have the longest pregnancy of any land mammal – 22 months! Babies are born knowing how to walk.
questions.hint: It's nearly 2 years!

questions.question: What do you call an animal that eats both plants and meat?
questions.explanation: Omnivores eat both plants and animals. Humans are omnivores! Bears and raccoons are too.
questions.hint: The prefix 'omni' means 'all'.

questions.question: What adaptation helps Arctic foxes survive extreme cold?
questions.explanation: Arctic foxes have a dense multi-layered fur coat that insulates them down to -58°F (-50°C)!
questions.hint: Their fur also changes color between summer and winter.

questions.question: How long does it take sunlight to reach Earth?
questions.explanation: Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to travel the 93 million miles to Earth!
questions.hint: It's less than 10 minutes.

questions.question: What is the name of the galaxy we live in?
questions.explanation: We live in the Milky Way galaxy – a spiral galaxy with about 200-400 billion stars!
questions.hint: It shares its name with a chocolate bar.

questions.question: Which planet has the most moons?
questions.explanation: Saturn has over 140 confirmed moons – more than any other planet! Jupiter comes in second.
questions.hint: It's also the planet with the most famous rings.

questions.question: What type of object is our Sun?
questions.explanation: Our Sun is classified as a yellow dwarf star – medium-sized and about 4.6 billion years old!
questions.hint: It's an average-sized star compared to the universe.

questions.question: What is a light-year?
choices: The distance light travels in one year
questions.explanation: A light-year is the distance light travels in one year – about 5.88 trillion miles!
questions.hint: It's a measure of distance, not time.

questions.question: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce energy for the cell through cellular respiration!
questions.hint: This is the most famous fact in biology class.

questions.question: What force keeps planets orbiting the Sun?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the attractive force between masses. The Sun's enormous gravity keeps all planets in orbit!
questions.hint: Isaac Newton famously studied this force – an apple inspired him.

questions.question: What is the pH of pure water?
questions.explanation: Pure water is neutral – pH 7. Below 7 is acidic, above 7 is alkaline!
questions.hint: It's right in the middle of the 0-14 pH scale.

questions.question: Which gas do plants absorb during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants absorb CO2 and release oxygen – which is why they're essential for our survival!
questions.hint: It's the same gas we breathe out.

questions.question: What travels faster: light or sound?
choices: They travel at the same speed
questions.explanation: Light travels at 186,000 miles per second. Sound is only 767 mph – that's why you see lightning before hearing thunder!
questions.hint: Think about the lightning-thunder gap in a storm.

questions.question: Who invented the telephone?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell is credited with inventing the telephone and making the first call in 1876!
questions.hint: His initials spell 'AGB'.

questions.question: When did World War II end?
questions.explanation: WWII ended in 1945: V-E Day (Victory in Europe) was May 8, and V-J Day (Japan) was September 2.
questions.hint: It was in the mid-1940s.

questions.question: Which ancient wonder still stands today?
questions.explanation: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the only original Ancient Wonder that still exists! Built around 2560 BC.
questions.hint: It's in Egypt.

questions.question: Who was the first US President?
questions.explanation: George Washington was the first President of the United States, serving from 1789 to 1797!
questions.hint: He's on the $1 bill.

questions.question: Where did the Renaissance begin?
questions.explanation: The Renaissance ('rebirth') began in Italy in the 14th century, focusing on art, science, and human potential!
questions.hint: Leonardo da Vinci was from this country.

questions.question: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River in Africa is approximately 4,130 miles long – the longest in the world!
questions.hint: It flows through Egypt.

questions.question: Which country has the most land area?
questions.explanation: Russia is the largest country in the world at about 6.6 million square miles – over double Canada!
questions.hint: It spans 11 time zones.

questions.question: What is the tallest mountain in the world?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest in the Himalayas is 29,032 feet above sea level – the world's highest point!
questions.hint: It borders Nepal and Tibet.

questions.question: Which is the smallest country in the world?
questions.explanation: Vatican City is only 0.17 square miles – an independent country within Rome, Italy!
questions.hint: It's the home of the Pope.

questions.question: In 'Avengers: Endgame', which character says 'I am Iron Man' at the end?
questions.explanation: Tony Stark says 'I am Iron Man' before snapping his fingers with the Infinity Gauntlet!
questions.hint: He's the one who started the whole Marvel universe.

questions.question: What is the name of the villain in 'The Lion King'?
questions.explanation: Scar is Simba's uncle who murders Mufasa to seize the Pride Lands from Simba!
questions.hint: He has a mark on his face.

questions.question: How many Infinity Stones are there in the Marvel Universe?
questions.explanation: There are 6 Infinity Stones: Space, Mind, Reality, Power, Time, and Soul!
questions.hint: One more than the fingers on one hand.

questions.question: Who wrote the Harry Potter book series?
questions.explanation: J.K. Rowling wrote all 7 Harry Potter books, starting with The Philosopher's Stone in 1997!
questions.hint: Her initials are her pen name.

questions.question: In which movie does a character say 'Why so serious?'
questions.explanation: The Joker (played by Heath Ledger) says 'Why so serious?' in The Dark Knight (2008)!
questions.hint: It's part of the Batman franchise.

questions.question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
questions.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup 5 times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002)!
questions.hint: They wear a famous yellow jersey.

questions.question: How often are the Summer Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The Summer Olympics are held every 4 years. The Winter Olympics also occur every 4 years, offset by 2!
questions.hint: This makes each athlete's window very precious.

questions.question: In tennis, what is it called when both players reach 40-40?
questions.explanation: 'Deuce' is when the score is tied at 40-40. A player must then win two consecutive points to win the game!
questions.hint: It's a French word that means 'two'.

questions.question: What sport uses a puck?
questions.explanation: Ice hockey uses a vulcanized rubber puck. Players can shoot it over 100 mph!
questions.hint: This sport is played on ice.

questions.question: What is the distance of a marathon?
questions.explanation: A marathon is exactly 26.2 miles (42.195 km). It traces back to a Greek soldier named Pheidippides!
questions.hint: It's longer than 25 miles.

questions.question: What is the world's most-visited website?
questions.explanation: Google is the most visited website in the world with billions of searches processed every day!
questions.hint: You use it to search for things.

questions.question: Which country is home to the kangaroo?
questions.explanation: Kangaroos are native to Australia – marsupials that carry their joeys in pouches!
questions.hint: It's a continent-country 'down under'.

questions.question: What is the main ingredient in guacamole?
questions.explanation: Guacamole is a Mexican dip made primarily from mashed avocado, often with lime juice and salt!
questions.hint: It's a green, creamy fruit.

questions.question: How many keys are on a standard piano?
questions.explanation: A standard piano has 88 keys – 52 white and 36 black, spanning over seven octaves!
questions.hint: It's between 85 and 90.

questions.question: What is the fastest land animal?
questions.explanation: The cheetah is the fastest land animal, reaching speeds up to 70 mph in short sprints!
questions.hint: It has distinctive black tear streaks on its face.

questions.question: What is the atomic number of carbon?
questions.explanation: Carbon has 6 protons in its nucleus, giving it atomic number 6. It's the basis of all life on Earth!
questions.hint: Count 6 protons.

questions.question: What does DNA stand for?
questions.explanation: DNA stands for Deoxyribonucleic Acid – the molecule that carries genetic instructions for life!
questions.hint: De-oxy-ribo-nucleic Acid.

questions.question: What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
choices: An object stays at rest or motion unless a net force acts on it
questions.explanation: Newton's First Law (Law of Inertia): Objects stay at rest or in motion unless a net external force acts on them!
questions.hint: It's about inertia.

questions.question: Which element is liquid at room temperature besides mercury?
questions.explanation: Bromine (Br) is one of only two elements that are liquid at room temperature. Mercury is the other!
questions.hint: It's a halogen element.

questions.question: What theory explains how the universe began?
questions.explanation: The Big Bang Theory proposes the universe began from an extremely hot dense state about 13.8 billion years ago!
questions.hint: It was also a popular TV show.

questions.question: What is the speed of light in a vacuum?
questions.explanation: Light travels at approximately 186,000 miles per second (300,000 km/s) in a vacuum – the fastest speed possible!
questions.hint: It's approximately 300,000 km/s.

questions.question: Which empire was the largest in history by land area?
questions.explanation: The British Empire was the largest in history – covering about 24% of Earth's total land area at its peak!
questions.hint: The sun never set on it.

questions.question: In what year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, marking the symbolic end of the Cold War!
questions.hint: It's the year before the 90s.

questions.question: Who developed the theory of general relativity?
questions.explanation: Albert Einstein published his General Theory of Relativity in 1915, revolutionizing our understanding of gravity and spacetime!
questions.hint: He's also famous for E=mc².

questions.question: Which country was the first to give women the right to vote?
questions.explanation: New Zealand became the first country to grant women the right to vote in 1893!
questions.hint: It's an island country near Australia.

questions.question: What ancient civilization built Machu Picchu?
questions.explanation: Machu Picchu was built by the Inca Empire in the 15th century in present-day Peru, high in the Andes Mountains!
questions.hint: Their empire stretched along the western coast of South America.

questions.question: What is the Tropic of Capricorn?
choices: The latitude 23.5 degrees South of the equator
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Capricorn is the latitude line at 23.5 degrees South – the southernmost point where the sun can be directly overhead!
questions.hint: Its companion is the Tropic of Cancer in the north.

questions.question: Which country has the longest coastline?
questions.explanation: Canada has the world's longest coastline at over 125,000 miles – due to its massive size and thousands of islands!
questions.hint: It's the second largest country by land area.

questions.question: What is the deepest point on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Challenger Deep in the Mariana Trench is about 36,000 feet (7 miles) below the Pacific Ocean!
questions.hint: It's named after islands nearby in the Pacific.

questions.question: Which two countries share the world's longest border?
questions.explanation: The USA-Canada border stretches about 5,525 miles, making it the longest international border in the world!
questions.hint: Both countries are in North America.

questions.question: What is a black hole?
choices: An area of very cold space
A region where gravity is so strong nothing escapes
questions.explanation: A black hole is a region in space where gravity is so intense that nothing – not even light – can escape!
questions.hint: Even light can't escape it.

questions.question: What is the name of the first artificial satellite launched into space?
questions.explanation: The Soviet Union launched Sputnik 1 in 1957, becoming the first artificial satellite to orbit Earth!
questions.hint: It was launched by the Soviet Union during the Cold War.

questions.question: What is the Hubble Space Telescope named after?
questions.explanation: Edwin Hubble discovered that galaxies exist beyond the Milky Way and that the universe is expanding!
questions.hint: He has a famous law named after him too.

questions.question: Which mission first landed humans on the Moon?
questions.explanation: Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on July 20, 1969. Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walked on the surface!
questions.hint: 11 is the one that worked – 13 famously had a problem.

questions.question: What is the cosmic microwave background radiation?
choices: Leftover radiation from the Big Bang
questions.explanation: CMB is thermal radiation left over from the early universe shortly after the Big Bang, detected uniformly across the sky!
questions.hint: It's like the universe's 'afterglow'.

questions.question: Which director made 'Schindler's List', 'Jurassic Park', and 'E.T.'?
questions.explanation: Steven Spielberg is one of cinema's greatest directors, responsible for dozens of iconic films across multiple genres!
questions.hint: His name starts with two S's.

questions.question: What is the highest-grossing film of all time (not adjusted for inflation)?
questions.explanation: Avatar (2009) holds the record with about $2.9 billion worldwide, after re-releases boosted it above Avengers: Endgame!
questions.hint: It involves blue aliens and was re-released multiple times.

questions.question: Which actress has won the most Academy Awards for Best Actress?
questions.explanation: Katharine Hepburn won 4 Academy Awards for Best Actress – a record that still stands today!
questions.hint: She was active from the 1930s through the 1980s.

questions.question: In 'Inception', what object does Cobb use to tell if he's dreaming?
questions.explanation: Cobb uses a spinning top as his totem: in dreams it spins forever; in reality it eventually falls!
questions.hint: If it keeps going... he's still dreaming.

questions.question: Who holds the record for the most home runs in MLB history?
questions.explanation: Barry Bonds holds the all-time MLB home run record with 762 career home runs!
questions.hint: He was a San Francisco Giant.

questions.question: In which sport would you perform a 'Fosbury Flop'?
questions.explanation: Dick Fosbury invented the Fosbury Flop at the 1968 Olympics – going over the bar backwards, revolutionizing high jump!
questions.hint: You go over a bar, but backwards.

questions.question: Who is widely considered the greatest basketball player of all time?
questions.explanation: Michael Jordan won 6 NBA championships with the Chicago Bulls and is widely considered the GOAT of basketball!
questions.hint: He wore #23 for the Chicago Bulls.

questions.question: How many Grand Slam tennis tournaments are there?
questions.explanation: There are 4 Grand Slams: Australian Open, French Open (Roland Garros), Wimbledon, and the US Open!
questions.hint: Think of the four seasons of the year.

questions.question: Which country invented the sport of cricket?
questions.explanation: Cricket originated in England in the 16th century. The first international cricket match was in 1844!
questions.hint: It's the country with Wimbledon and the Premier League.

questions.question: What is the Fibonacci sequence?
choices: Each number is double the last
Each number is the sum of the two before it
Each number is the square of its position
questions.explanation: The Fibonacci sequence: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13... Each number is the sum of the two preceding it. It appears throughout nature!

questions.question: Who wrote 'Romeo and Juliet'?
questions.explanation: William Shakespeare wrote Romeo and Juliet around 1594. It's the world's most famous tragic love story!
questions.hint: He also wrote Hamlet and Othello.

questions.question: What is the longest word in the English language?
questions.explanation: Pneumonoultramicroscopicsilicovolcanoconiosis (45 letters) is a lung disease caused by silica dust – the longest word in a major dictionary!
questions.hint: It's a lung disease name.

questions.question: How many chambers does a human heart have?
questions.explanation: The human heart has 4 chambers: left and right atria (upper) and left and right ventricles (lower)!
questions.hint: It's an even number greater than 3.

questions.question: What country consumes the most chocolate per capita?
questions.explanation: Switzerland consistently tops the charts for chocolate consumption – about 10-11 kg per person per year!
questions.hint: Swiss chocolate is world-famous.
